title: Agents page: cross-agent dependency and queue visibility panel
description:
The `/agents` health page shows each agent's status independently (idle/in_progress/blocked/error). It doesn't show which tasks are queued for each agent, which tasks are blocking other agents, or cross-agent dependency chains.

Add a 'Queue & Blockers' panel below the agent health cards on the `/agents` page that shows:
1. **Per-agent queue**: for each agent, list tasks with status `ready` or `in_progress` — task_id, title (truncated), priority badge
2. **Blocked tasks**: tasks with `status: blocked` grouped by `blocked_by` — show what's blocking them (task ID + current status of the blocker)
3. **Cross-agent waits**: highlight when Agent A is idle but waiting for Agent B to complete a dependency (e.g. engineer idle, review task blocked on `github:pr-not-merged`)

Data source: extend the `/api/agent/stats` response to include `queuedTasks: [{task_id, title, status, priority}]` and `blockedTasks: [{task_id, title, blocked_by: string[]}]` per agent, populated from `getCanonicalTasks()` (already called in `writeHealthSnapshots`).

UI: collapsible section per agent, collapsed by default, max 5 tasks shown with 'show more' link to Kanban.
handoff_notes:
[agent:engineer][task:dashboard-0049] implemented and reconciled to durable dashboard history.

Durable implementation landed on dashboard `main` via commit `000f816`.

What actually shipped:
- src/lib/control/health.ts now includes `queued_tasks` and `blocked_tasks` in the health snapshot model derived from canonical task state
- src/app/agents/page.tsx renders a collapsible `Queue & Blockers` panel on each agent card using the existing `/api/control/health` data path
- the shipped UI shows queued tasks with priority and status labels, blocked tasks with their first blocker, and a link to `/kanban` when more items exist than the inline limit

What did not land durably as part of this task:
- the current local-only work in src/app/api/agent/stats/route.ts
- the local-only endpoint src/app/api/canonical/tasks/route.ts
- the previously claimed commit `d6fe008` does not exist in the durable dashboard repository state and was removed from task metadata

Validation:
- git history check confirmed `000f816` as the durable implementation commit
- live dashboard `/agents` continues to render the Queue & Blockers panel after the rendering fix and service restart
- current dashboard build passes after the later health typing fix

Durable commit:
- 000f816 (`feat(agents): add Queue & Blockers panel to agent health cards`)

artifacts.description: Added queued_tasks and blocked_tasks to the durable health snapshot model derived from canonical task state
Added a collapsible Queue & Blockers panel per agent card, including queued task priority/status display and blocked task visibility
